content.claim: School closure or merger can trigger 'white flight' and increase between-school segregation in mixed communities, though the effect is context-dependent and contested; conversely, maintaining small single-class-per-grade schools has historically limited within-school segregation.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Literature explicitly treats segregation effects as context-dependent rather than uniform
Causal attribution of segregation changes to closure decisions versus other factors remains contested in research
Encs case study is illustrative but not nationally generalizable
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
